title: xrtm Forecast Object Standard (v1.1)
description: Standardized schema for high-intelligence forecasting. v1.1 adds optional parametric distribution support for epistemic uncertainty.

metadata.description: Operational and contextual metadata for the forecast record.

id.description: Unique identifier for this specific forecast instance.

created_at.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp when the forecast was generated.

snapshot_time.description: Zero Leakage: The specific 'Time T' at which the world state was frozen for this forecast. No data after this timestamp should have been available to the model.

source_version.description: The version of the inference engine (xrtm-forecast) or model used.

id.description: Unique identifier for the underlying question.

title.description: The core question or statement being forecasted.

description.description: Detailed context, background, and specific definitions.

resolution_criteria.description: The explicit, non-ambiguous rules for how the ground truth will be determined.

probability.description: The assigned probability of the primary outcome occurring. If distribution is provided, this MUST equal distribution.mean().

distribution.description: Optional parametric probability distribution representing epistemic uncertainty. When provided, 'probability' must equal the distribution's expected value.

family.description: Distribution family. 'beta' is recommended for binary outcomes.

alpha.description: Beta shape parameter α (required if family='beta'). Represents 'Yes-weighted' pseudo-count.

beta.description: Beta shape parameter β (required if family='beta'). Represents 'No-weighted' pseudo-count.

std.description: Distribution standard deviation (used for gaussian_bounded family).

low.description: Lower bound of the credible interval.

high.description: Upper bound of the credible interval.

confidence_interval.description: DEPRECATED in v1.1. Use distribution.credible_interval instead. Retained for backward compatibility.

level.description: The confidence level (e.g., 0.9 for a 90% interval).

reasoning_trace.description: The Bayesian-style sequence of assumptions and causal logic.

narrative.description: A comprehensive narrative argument supporting the forecast.

causal_graph.description: The structured Mental Model representing the causal logic.

event.description: An assumption or intermediate event in the reasoning chain.

weight.description: The conditional probability or causal strength.

calibration_metrics.description: Self-assessed or system-derived metrics for probabilistic integrity.

self_calibration_score.description: The model's own estimate of how calibrated it is on this specific domain.
